slides.title: Adding PQ capabilities to SSH protocol

polls.text: do people think taking on the ssh sig work is a reasonable thing?

chat.author: Damien Miller
chat.text: hello!

chat.author: Bas Westerbaan
chat.text: :wave:

chat.author: Caspar Schutijser
chat.text: Hi

chat.author: Damien Miller
chat.text: please no crossposting wars

chat.author: Bas Westerbaan
chat.text: You're absolutely right! Great catch.

chat.author: Damien Miller
chat.text: the agent draft has been around for 10yr, the protocol for ~25

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: I was going to say, I thought I was in my 30's when ssh agent showed up...

chat.author: Damien Miller
chat.text: oh no, it's not _that_ old

chat.author: Deb Cooley
chat.text: How old do you think Bas is?

chat.author: Simon Josefsson
chat.text: re chacha20 draft, the draft was refresh and I'm not aware of any outstanding issues beyond the bigger question if publishing it is a good idea  (which I am biased towards doing)

chat.author: Deb Cooley
chat.text: Oh no, that was Bob!!!

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: I am certainly that old.

chat.author: Sean Turner
chat.text: Love the SSH Certificate Format I-D. I have already used it to show people that an SSH certificate ain't a X.509 Certificate ;)

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: I'll have to tell damien it needs more X.509 in it. (ducks)

chat.author: Sean Turner
chat.text: yeah don't ;)

chat.author: Damien Miller
chat.text: "non spicy category"

chat.author: John Preuß Mattsson
chat.text: The authors were Igoe and Solinas

chat.author: Sean Turner
chat.text: Shepherd write-up should note the downref and a call it a day.

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: we make sure it works and is accurate - chairs do something to make whatever ietf spankings happen happen?

chat.author: Sean Turner
chat.text: :rolling_on_the_floor_laughing:

chat.author: Simon Josefsson
chat.text: sorry audio is not going to work

chat.author: Thom Wiggers
chat.text: I see at least Ticket to Ride (which is a great game)

chat.author: Simon Josefsson
chat.text: this is really just documenting existing format

chat.author: Deb Cooley
chat.text: @bob - hey!

chat.author: Thom Wiggers
chat.text: but this is outside the SSH protocol though?

chat.author: Simon Josefsson
chat.text: what damien said. +1 that git is supporting this for many years

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: yeah it's been on git for many years.

chat.author: Thom Wiggers
chat.text: (I don't think we shouldn't do it)

chat.author: Sean Turner
chat.text: do it

chat.author: Thom Wiggers
chat.text: there may be different security requirements from the regular SSH protocol...

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: while I'm a yes, thom is very correct that the requirements may be different.

chat.author: Damien Miller
chat.text: weasel words ftw

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: which is the reason to do it and ensure the security considerations section appropriately addresses how any dichotomy might affect each use.

chat.author: Simon Josefsson
chat.text: are the requirements for ssh known/documented?  there were some chat in cfrg about the EUF vs SUF requirements, but my take was that it was still not clear

chat.author: Damien Miller
chat.text: fwiw I think examining differing security requirements for detached signatures is something we should absolutely examine in the WG

chat.author: Thom Wiggers
chat.text: I think EUF-CMA should be fine, but I flagged it with the authors of the proofs for SSH

chat.author: Thom Wiggers
chat.text: EUF-CMA secure signatures are malleable  though, which may have side effects if you consider arbitrary applications (e.g., some system that assumes that any signed message only appears once)

chat.author: Thom Wiggers
chat.text: but that may just be a security considerations point

chat.author: Job Snijders
chat.text: please send this to the list!

chat.author: Sophie Schmieg
chat.text: can we discuss it in less than 100 emails?

chat.author: Stephen Farrell
chat.text: ok 99 then:-)

chat.author: Job Snijders
chat.text: (good feedback on EUF vs SUF, but mailing list really is the best place)

chat.author: Thom Wiggers
chat.text: strict upper limit: if a thread goes over 100 the authors and chairs get to decide what they want to do

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: maybe when my mail server stops falling over from recieving the TLS list..

chat.author: Thom Wiggers
chat.text: Job: yeah I'll send it when the thread appears

chat.author: John Preuß Mattsson
chat.text:
Thom Wiggers wrote:

but that may just be a security considerations point

I don't think that approach work very well. If you can I think you should make it hard to misuse. Requirements like using unpredictable IVs, doing point validation, etc. has led to major vulnerabilities.

chat.author: Simon Josefsson
chat.text: -1 on adding more mldsa+eddsa variants - IMHO one is good enough to solve that problem space

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: So would adopting these be a re-charter?

chat.author: Bas Westerbaan
chat.text: I don't care as long as there is one obvious choice.

chat.author: Dmitry Belyavskiy
chat.text: I think we will need different levels of ML-DSA

chat.author: Sophie Schmieg
chat.text: +1 to Damien, please have both adopted

chat.author: Stephen Farrell
chat.text: @bas: that'd be good said at mic, want me to do it for you or...?

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: Just pick a single one of each

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: let's not say we will keep it simple and end up with 18

chat.author: Sophie Schmieg
chat.text: honestly, there are so few SUF use cases that I would rather have less options than having another SUF one

chat.author: Thom Wiggers
chat.text: @John: those concerns do not relate to SUF-CMA or EUF-CMA

chat.author: Mike Ounsworth
chat.text: +1 to John Mattsson: standardizing pure ML-DSA now doesn't mean that implementations have to -support- enable it now; but it does mean that they can be prepared for when they do want to turn it on.

chat.author: Simon Josefsson
chat.text: i think we want some non-MLDSA option as fallback. SLH-DSA solves this

chat.author: Mike Ounsworth
chat.text: I agree with Damien that only mldsa44 hybrids should be fine from a security analysis perspective. But I disagree from a compliance perspective. If you have some security policy requiring, for example, lvl 3+, then whether that policy is misguided or not is irrelevant -- not having software that satisfies it will be a frustration point for operators.

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: I for one intend for my ssh key to resist our new dyson sphere building overlords...

chat.author: Bas Westerbaan
chat.text:
Simon Josefsson said:

i think we want some non-MLDSA option as fallback. SLH-DSA solves this

With what key agreement would you pair that?

chat.author: Sophie Schmieg
chat.text:
Bob Beck said:

t our new dyson sphere building ove

are you quoting Google internal documents I may or may not have written?

chat.author: Damien Miller
chat.text: fwiw we do have two standardised pq kex for ssh already

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: heavens no.

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: only the simpsons.

chat.author: Damien Miller
chat.text: sophie, please do not discuss the dyson swarm proposal. nor the goat teleporter

chat.author: Simon Josefsson
chat.text:
Bas Westerbaan said:

Simon Josefsson said:

i think we want some non-MLDSA option as fallback. SLH-DSA solves this

With what key agreement would you pair that?

key agreement is entirely orthogonal to signatures, no?  we already have sntrup/mlkem+x25519

chat.author: Nicola Tuveri
chat.text: if we do not wait for cfrg, would it be ok to pursue compat with the lamps composite-sigs?

chat.author: Damien Miller
chat.text:
Nicola Tuveri said:

if we do not wait for cfrg, would it be ok to pursue compat with the lamps composite-sigs?

imo yes, that's draft-miller-sshm-mldsa44-ed25519-composite-sigs

chat.author: Simon Josefsson
chat.text:
Damien Miller said:

Nicola Tuveri said:

if we do not wait for cfrg, would it be ok to pursue compat with the lamps composite-sigs?

imo yes, that's draft-miller-sshm-mldsa44-ed25519-composite-sigs

yes -- i think that solves the mldsa+ecc hybrid problem space.  i don't see we need more alternatives for solving that.

chat.author: Bas Westerbaan
chat.text:
key agreement is entirely orthogonal to signatures, no? we already have sntrup/mlkem+x25519

Both need to be secure.

chat.author: Thom Wiggers
chat.text: both meaning one of each right?

chat.author: Rich Salz
chat.text: yes

chat.author: Bas Westerbaan
chat.text: The key agreement and the authentication.

chat.author: Thom Wiggers
chat.text: (my question being re: "adopt both")

chat.author: Rich Salz
chat.text: adopt both that scott has on pg4 of his slide

chat.author: Simon Josefsson
chat.text: imho people who want multiple ML-DSA security levels can go for pure ML-DSA options.

chat.author: Thom Wiggers
chat.text: he has "either" twice :P

chat.author: Damien Miller
chat.text:
Mike Ounsworth said:

I agree with Damien that only mldsa44 hybrids should be fine from a security analysis perspective. But I disagree from a compliance perspective. If you have some security policy requiring, for example, lvl 3+, then whether that policy is misguided or not is irrelevant -- not having software that satisfies it will be a frustration point for operators.

ok, so if we pick one ML-DSA variant (87?) and one classical signature (ECDSA p384?) would that work?

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: sure.

chat.author: Rich Salz
chat.text: I meant "both" as in scott's two bullets, and yes one of the bullets has a decision point.

chat.author: Dmitry Belyavskiy
chat.text: I agree with Simon

chat.author: Mike Ounsworth
chat.text:
Damien Miller said:

ok, so if we pick one ML-DSA variant (87?) and one classical signature (ECDSA p384?) would that work?

I mean, I'm the guy responsible for the 18 choices in LAMPS because I think that codepoints are cheap. So maybe don't ask me?

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: someone get me some duct tape for mike

chat.author: Simon Josefsson
chat.text: i still believe we need something as a backup to ML-DSA, which seems to be lost here

chat.author: Damien Miller
chat.text: that was not meant to be shade but ...

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: I beleve we previously mentioned SLH-DSA

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: but not here

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: (that was for simon)

chat.author: Thom Wiggers
chat.text: SLH-DSA is fine too, and we can just avoid any hybrids

chat.author: Mike Ounsworth
chat.text:
Bob Beck said:

I beleve we previously mentioned SLH-DSA

But was that sufficiently reflected in minutes, etc that there is demand for such a draft to exist?

chat.author: Sophie Schmieg
chat.text: yeah SLH-DSA does not even need to be hybridized if you want to follow BSI guidance

chat.author: Stephen Farrell
chat.text: @simon: I think we just agreed that we'd consider things like SLH_DSA at an interim after we've adopted initial sig drafts

chat.author: Jonathan Hammell
chat.text: Others (like SLH-DSA) were going to be discussed in the interim meeting, I thought.

chat.author: Job Snijders
chat.text:
so i am hearing "3", eventually

Pure ML-DSA (phase 1)
ML-DSA hybrid w/ classic (phase 1)
SLH-DSA  (phase 2)

?

chat.author: Simon Josefsson
chat.text: my preference would be to do three things: 1) adopt mldsa44+ed25519 miller draft, 2) adopt scott's pure ml-dsa draft (3 levels), 3) adopt slh-dsa (which could be pure or hybrid)

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: that.

chat.author: Thom Wiggers
chat.text: Job: sounds accurate

chat.author: Sophie Schmieg
chat.text: yes, that

chat.author: Mike Ounsworth
chat.text:
Simon Josefsson said:

my preference would be to do three things: 1) adopt mldsa44+ed25519 miller draft, 2) adopt scott's pure ml-dsa draft (3 levels), 3) adopt slh-dsa (which could be pure or hybrid)

That, except don't even consider slh-dsa in hybrid.

chat.author: Damien Miller
chat.text: SEED

chat.author: Simon Josefsson
chat.text: the combinatorial explosion for slh-dsa is problematic.  guidance/discussion which variants to pick would be useful

chat.author: Sophie Schmieg
chat.text: with the SLH-DSA being pure, there really is no point in hybridizing SLH-DSA, as it has a reduction to the hash function

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: SEED

chat.author: Nicola Tuveri
chat.text: please, seeds -only everywhere!

chat.author: Thom Wiggers
chat.text: kill expanded key with fire

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: SEED

chat.author: Damien Miller
chat.text: I have a sticker that says SEED

chat.author: Deb Cooley
chat.text: Even pure SLH-DSA has options....

chat.author: David Benjamin
chat.text: SEED

chat.author: Simon Josefsson
chat.text:
Sophie Schmieg said:

with the SLH-DSA being pure, there really is no point in hybridizing SLH-DSA, as it has a reduction to the hash function

hedging implementation issues isn't unreasonable: even slh-dsa + ml-dsa in hybrid seems possible

chat.author: David Benjamin
chat.text: I'm so tired of the interop nightmare lamps produced

chat.author: Thom Wiggers
chat.text: SLH-DSA: I would say just use the large -f variants, and just SHA2 since  that's what is already used by SSH afaik

chat.author: Ben S
chat.text: Instructions unclear, implemented SEED

chat.author: Simon Josefsson
chat.text:
Thom Wiggers said:

SLH-DSA: I would say just use the large -f variants, and just SHA2 since  that's what is already used by SSH afaik

some people strongly oppose sha2. some people don't have access to shake....

chat.author: John Preuß Mattsson
chat.text:
For SLH-DSA you probable want the 128 variant. It is conservative anyway. You want the f variant as there is online signing. And I think everything new should be SHA-3.
So SLH-DSA-SHAKE-128f

chat.author: Bas Westerbaan
chat.text: I don't see the point of SLH-DSA without having a key agreement to pair it with. If all lattices are broken, then lattice SSH with SLH-DSA is still insecure.

chat.author: Mike Ounsworth
chat.text: Minutes: Scott proposes a question on-list: "Can anyone not live with seed-only?"

chat.author: Sophie Schmieg
chat.text: speaking of discussing things in 100 emails or less

chat.author: Damien Miller
chat.text: we need SHAKE for ML-KEM and ML-DSA, so many implementations will carry it for those

chat.author: Mike Ounsworth
chat.text:
Bas Westerbaan said:

I don't see the point of SLH-DSA without having a key agreement to pair it with. If all lattices are broken, then lattice SSH with SLH-DSA is still insecure.

Don't SSH keys have sign-only use cases, like signing git commits, signing firmware packages, etc?

chat.author: Simon Josefsson
chat.text:
Bas Westerbaan said:

I don't see the point of SLH-DSA without having a key agreement to pair it with. If all lattices are broken, then lattice SSH with SLH-DSA is still insecure.

signature algorithms aren't paired with key agreement in SSH.  Are you arguing for classic mceliece?

chat.author: John Preuß Mattsson
chat.text:
Bas wrote:

I don't see the point of SLH-DSA without having a key agreement to pair it with. If all lattices are broken, then lattice SSH with SLH-DSA is still insecure.

That is a good point.

chat.author: Sophie Schmieg
chat.text: kex can be negogiated ephemerally, identity keys can not

chat.author: Sophie Schmieg
chat.text: you can always retrofit the kex, in the unlikely scenario of an MLWE break

chat.author: David Benjamin
chat.text: Please. I implemented GSS-API in JavaScript. It's totally modern. :upside_down:

chat.author: Mike Ounsworth
chat.text:
Sophie Schmieg said:

kex can be negogiated ephemerally, identity keys can not

More to the point: signature pub keys get stuck in very non-ephemeral, non-trivial-to-roll, places like in the .ssh/ dir of a server, or when I TOFU some server's key.

chat.author: Damien Miller
chat.text: no objection to adoption of GSSAPI hybrid KEX. It's not something OpenSSH intends to implement but I recognise that there does exist a user community that likes it

chat.author: Thom Wiggers
chat.text: Mike: I presume you mean s/pub keys/authentication keys/

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: yep

chat.author: Thom Wiggers
chat.text: of course everything here is pkc

chat.author: Sophie Schmieg
chat.text: Kerberos is already PQC

chat.author: Alexey Melnikov
chat.text: Yes

chat.author: Bob Beck
chat.text: https://www.imperialviolet.org/2024/04/07/letskerberos.html

chat.author: Dmitry Belyavskiy
chat.text: @Sophie but PKINIT isn't :)

chat.author: Dmitry Belyavskiy
chat.text: Damien, thank you!

chat.author: Simon Josefsson
chat.text: i guess nobody cares about hybrid SUF-CMA for ML-DSA then?  that's the conclusion if we adopt miller+scott drafts

chat.author: Damien Miller
chat.text: [I don't]

chat.author: Sophie Schmieg
chat.text: I don't care about SUF, ever

chat.author: Alicja Kario
chat.text: I don't care about it either

chat.author: Nicola Tuveri
chat.text: we can have the hybryd-SUF after CFRG finishes, but in the meantime we can have what works from lamps

chat.author: Alicja Kario
chat.text: yup, what Nikola said

chat.author: Mike Ounsworth
chat.text: At the risk of airing my own ignorance, I don't even understand what kind of thing would care that strongly about the EUF vs SUF difference ... (other than closing some proof in some paper)

chat.author: Alicja Kario
chat.text: if SUF-CMA turns out to be important for hybrids, we can add them later, I don't think we benefit from delaying publishing hybrids to be able to have SUF-CMA

chat.author: Damien Miller
chat.text:
Mike Ounsworth said:

At the risk of airing my own ignorance, I don't even understand what kind of thing would care that strongly about the EUF vs SUF difference ... (other than closing some proof in some paper)

mtgox apparently

chat.author: Nicola Tuveri
chat.text: +1 for both Mike and Alicja

chat.author: Simon Josefsson
chat.text: we know SUF-CMA is relevant for standalone SSH signature use-cases like git and firmware signing

chat.author: Damien Miller
chat.text:
Damien Miller said:

mtgox apparently

https://arxiv.org/abs/1403.6676

chat.author: Mike Ounsworth
chat.text:
Simon Josefsson said:

we know SUF-CMA is relevant for standalone SSH signature use-cases like git and firmware signing

I genuinely would like to learn about this. Is there a paper / blog / something somewhere?

chat.author: Job Snijders
chat.text: thanks all!!!!

chat.author: Alicja Kario
chat.text: I have doubts about the SUF-CMA needed git use-case...

chat.author: Job Snijders
chat.text: thanks for staying up late Damien :-)

chat.author: Damien Miller
chat.text: thanks chairs for running a [almost entirely] drama-free wg

chat.author: Nicola Tuveri
chat.text: /popcorn
